items.title: Jewish worshippers allegedly targeted in fake pistol incident in Sydney

children.content: Two men have been charged after allegedly pointing a fake gun at a Double Bay synagogue, six months after the Bondi Beach terrorist attack.

image.altText: Man with grey blazer and blue cap looking at camera

items.title: Woman denied bail after child found dead in NSW Central Coast home

children.content: The body of a four-year-old child has been found in a home in Wyong on the NSW Central Coast.

image.altText: a police officer stands next to a police van at the site of an alleged murder at wyong

items.title: Student balloons capture Earth images from 35 kilometres above Cobar

children.content: Students from Newcastle head to outback NSW to launch two balloons into the Earth's upper atmosphere.

gallery.altText: a picture of the earth from space
group photo of high school students

thumbnail.altText: a picture of the earth from space
group photo of high school students

items.title: Man's body pulled from Murray River at Barooga

children.content: NSW Police are investigating the death of a man who was found in the Murray River.

image.altText: Images of blue and white checkered police tape stretched across a suburban street.

items.title: Knights end Dolphins' win streak with gripping one-point win in Newcastle

children.content: The Knights end the red-hot Dolphins' eight-game winning streak thanks to a Dylan Brown field goal, after forgotten half Jonah Pezet stars in a 23-14 win for the Eels over Manly. Follow live.

image.altText: A group of rugby league players in red and blue stripes celebrate a win, hugging each other

items.title: Residents farewell frozen bread and long-life milk as supermarket opens

children.content: This community now no longer needs to travel 100+ kilometres to get their groceries.

gallery.altText: Man and a woman standing in a supermarket, smiling and holding a black basket

thumbnail.altText: Man and a woman standing in a supermarket, smiling and holding a black basket

items.title: Sydney Trains female apprentices step up amid 94pc gender skew

children.content: Nicola Huggins and Ariana Gackowski are learning the wiring, welding and hand tool skills required to one day repair the trains Sydneysiders travel on every day.

image.altText: Ariana Gackowski (right) and Nicola Huggins in bright orange hazmat suits.

items.title: Dad and daughter defy doubters on epic voyage from Newcastle to Norway

children.content: A NSW family is returning a wooden yacht to its roots in Norway, but it has not been all smooth sailing.

image.altText: A father and daughter, both wearing beanies, with the ocean behind them.

items.title: Pay increase the final straw for struggling small businesses

children.content: Small businesses struggling with rising costs say a recent wage increase is the final straw. In one case, the owner of a corner store that has been running for 70 years decided to close the business.

gallery.altText: A woman standing outside a store with her hand on the front door with a closed sign

thumbnail.altText: A woman standing outside a store with her hand on the front door with a closed sign

items.title: First case of deadly H5N1 bird flu confirmed in NSW

children.content: The first case of the deadly H5 bird flu is confirmed in a giant petrel found on the NSW Mid North Coast.

articles.title: New bird flu advice for egg producers as testing continues

children.content: States and territories are urged to encourage free-range producers to keep their birds indoors where practical in an effort to combat bird flu.

image.altText: A brown chicken looks at the camera. Lots more chickens are in the background.

gallery.altText: Bennetts Beach NSW Mid North Coast

thumbnail.altText: Bennetts Beach NSW Mid North Coast

items.title: Two Sydney Mardi Gras board members removed after months of disputes

children.content: Damien Nguyen and Luna Choo from Pride in Protest were voted off the Sydney Gay and Lesbian Mardi Gras board by a 70 per cent majority.

image.altText: Luna Choo and Damien Nguyen hold a trans flag

items.title: Government rejects all UN recommendations on LGBTQIA+ rights

children.content: The Albanese government has accepted the lowest number of recommendations made by the UN Human Rights Council in 20 years, with none protecting the LGBTQIA+ community.

image.altText: albanese, with his partner, waves as he walks in a parade of people holding rainbow flags

items.title: Tiny forests planted in Sydney to restore urban tree canopy

children.content: Using enriched soil and densely planted trees, the pocket forests can fit into small underutilised spaces owned by councils.

gallery.altText: a small forest surrounded by grass as seen from above

thumbnail.altText: a small forest surrounded by grass as seen from above

items.title: How NAIDOC grew from a one-day protest to a week-long celebration

children.content: Traditionally, NAIDOC Week was celebrated by First Nations people, but it has become an invitation for non-Indigenous Australians to get involved.

image.altText: A montage of images of NAIDOC protests and image of Day of Mourning with Indigenous leaders in 1938

items.title: 'Taking control': Manufacturing of trains to return to the Hunter

children.content: NSW Premier Chris Minns tells a conference of the party faithful that the state's next set of trains will be manufactured "where the original fleet was built".

items.title: 'Towelling the bloke up in the cells': Shocking police emails revealed

children.content: Emails reveal an extraordinary exchange between AFP officers after an Indigenous man was allegedly beaten and illegally strip-searched at a Canberra watchhouse.

gallery.altText: A person's face and police tape and bars in a graphic image.

thumbnail.altText: A person's face and police tape and bars in a graphic image.

items.title: How rugby league became Australia's most valuable football code

children.content: A $5 billion-plus broadcast rights deal is expected to be announced this week, making rugby league Australia's most valuable football code.

items.title: Rethinking drinking as gen Z wakes up to joy of early rising

children.content: Pub nights are becoming far less rowdy as young people swap big nights out for morning run clubs.

image.altText: Crowds outside of Romano's Pub on New Years Eve in the 90s

items.title: 'An insult': How AI entered Sydney's underground music scene

children.content: Local musician Aidan Sammut says he was disgusted after a live music event aimed at showcasing local artists included an artist who used AI-generated content.

image.altText: A man in his mid fifties performs on stage under a purple spotlight.

items.title: Farmers turn restaurant food scraps into 'very valuable commodities'

children.content: While diners enjoy their seaside meals, Andrew Collins is happily outside chasing the scraps.

image.altText: A close up of two hands holding a scoop of soil rich with active worms

items.title: Endangered pine forest grown with people's cremated ashes opens

children.content: When 10-year-old Tegan Chen died after accidentally slipping into a rushing creek on a walk, it stirred her parents to set up "living memorials" for others.

gallery.altText: Woman stands surounded by wollemi pine saplings with sandstone cliffs in the background.
drone vision of house, cliffs and lots of little saplings planted in paddocks.
Woman stands surounded by wollemi pine saplings with sandstone cliffs in the background.
